summary: Matched the common fighter down-state file `ftCo_Down.c` and reorganized nearby `ftCo_09F4`/`ftCo_09F7` code and data out of the large `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` bucket. The PR removed inline assembly from `ftCo_Down_CheckInput` and `ftCo_80098324`, leaving C that reports 100% for all functions in `ftCo_Down.c`. It also split remaining address-range work into new source/asm/header files, moved shared GObj extern declarations into `gobj.h`, and folded `ftdynamics` data into the relevant ftCommon area for ordering/matching.
what_changed: `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Down.c` went from inline assembly for key functions to C for `ftCo_Down_CheckInput`, `ftCo_80098324`, `ftCo_80098400`, and the Down callbacks; the PR report shows the file at 0/17600 score, 100.00%.
`ftCo_80098324` retained a small `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` unused stack padding array (`u8 _[8] = { 0 };`) before calling `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, `ftAnim_8006EBA4`, and `ftCommon_8007CCE8`.
`ftCo_80098400` was tightened to take `ftCo_GObj*` instead of `Fighter_GObj*`.
Large chunks of assembly for the 0x8009F4A4+ range were removed from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` and redistributed into `ftCo_09F4.c`, `ftCo_09F7.c`, and small asm files for data sections.
Added `asm/melee/ft/ftCo_09F4.s` for `.sbss` globals `ftCo_804D6568`, `ftCo_804D656C` and `.sdata` objects/strings around `ftCo_804D3B48`, `ftCo_804D3B50`, `ftCo_804D3B58`, and `ftCo_804D3B60`.

`ftCo_09F4.c` gained headers for ft, ground, lb, baselib gobj helpers, lobj, and trig/math; it contains one matched C function `ftCo_8009F480` and several `ASM UNK_RET` fallback bodies for still-unmatched functions when `MUST_MATCH` is active.

`ftdynamics.c` and `ftdynamics.h` were deleted; data including `ftDynamics_assert_msg_0`, `ftDynamics_803C5780`, and `ftDynamics_803C5794` was moved into `ftCo_09F4.c` under MWERKS/force-active guarded code.

`obj_files.mk` stopped building `src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c.o` and started building the new `asm/melee/ft/ftCo_09CB.s.o`, `asm/melee/ft/ftCo_09F4.s.o`, and `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_09F7.c.o` entries.

Common declarations for `HSD_GObj_80408610`, `HSD_GObj_80408620`, and `HSD_GObj_804D7848`-`HSD_GObj_804D784A` were moved to `src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h`, allowing duplicate local externs to be removed from `gobj.c`, `fighter.c`, `ground.c`, `grlib.c`, `ifstatus.c`, and `item.c`.
smart_moves: The PR isolated exact data-section ordering problems into small asm files rather than forcing unrelated source files to own sdata/sbss symbols prematurely.
It matched `ftCo_Down.c` incrementally while leaving difficult neighboring functions as `ASM UNK_RET` wrappers, avoiding regression from trying to decompile too broad an address range at once.
The unused stack padding in `ftCo_80098324` is an explicit, localized matching shim and is annotated with `@todo`, making the remaining compiler artifact easy to find later.
Centralizing GObj externs in `gobj.h` reduced duplicated local extern declarations across unrelated gameplay systems and made subsequent includes more accurate.
Moving the `ftdynamics` constants/data into `ftCo_09F4.c` appears to preserve object/link order while deleting a tiny standalone source object that no longer fit the build layout.
decomp_lessons: When a large asm bucket like `ft_08A4.s` is being retired, split both text and data deliberately: code can move to address-range C files while stubborn `.sdata`/`.sbss` symbols stay in small asm companions.
A function can be considered matched even if it needs a tiny stack-shaping local under `MUST_MATCH`; keep that shim narrow and documented.
Header cleanup is part of matching work: changing callers to include `ftCo_09F4.h` or `ftCo_09F7.h` prevents future files from depending on broad catch-all headers such as `ft_08A4.h`.
Central extern declarations belong in the owning subsystem header once multiple files use them; local extern duplicates become noise and can hide type mismatches.
Deleting or moving small data-only source files must be coordinated with `obj_files.mk` so object order and section placement remain controlled.
naming_conventions: Address-range files use `ftCo_09F4` and `ftCo_09F7` naming when semantic names are not yet known.
The matched semantic state file remains `ftCo_Down.c`/`ftCo_Down.h`, with callbacks named `ftCo_Down_Anim`, `ftCo_Down_IASA`, `ftCo_Down_Phys`, and `ftCo_Down_Coll`.
The header guard for `ftCo_Down.h` changed from a path-style guard to an address-style guard, `GALE01_097D9C`.
Use `ftCo_GObj*` for common fighter callbacks where the function belongs to ftCommon, rather than the broader `Fighter_GObj*` spelling.
New headers such as `ftCo_09F4.h` and `ftCo_09F7.h` carry declarations for address-named functions like `ftCo_8009F4A4`, `ftCo_8009F578`, `ftCo_8009F7F8`, `ftCo_8009F834`, and `ftCo_800A0098`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `ASM UNK_RET ... #if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP) { NOT_IMPLEMENTED; } #else { nofralloc ... }` to keep unmatched functions buildable while preserving exact assembly under matching builds.
Used `#pragma peephole on` after inline assembly blocks in `ftCo_09F4.c`.
Used `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO` and `#pragma force_active on` around constants/data that need compiler/linker preservation.
Placed hard-to-match globals in dedicated asm files with explicit `.section .sdata`, `.section .sbss`, `.balign`, `.global`, `.asciz`, `.4byte`, and `.skip` directives.
For `ftCo_80098324`, inserted an 8-byte unused stack local under `MUST_MATCH` to reproduce the original frame while leaving the logic as normal C.
Used direct C includes for specific dependencies (`gobjgxlink.h`, `gobjobject.h`, `gobjproc.h`, `lobj.h`, `MSL/trigf.h`) rather than relying only on placeholder declarations.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no explicit reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that replace the `ASM UNK_RET` bodies in `ftCo_09F4.c` and `ftCo_09F7.c` with C.
Check whether `ftDynamics_803C57B0` or other deleted `ftdynamics` symbols reappear elsewhere in subsequent object-order cleanups.
Search for additional conversions from path-style header guards to `GALE01_` address-style guards in ftCommon headers.
Find later cleanup of the `u8 _[8]` unused stack shim in `ftCo_80098324`.
